notes: Planned to Savings 'actual' now reflects the NET cash that truly moved into the savings account each month, not just explicit transfers. Previously the row's actual summed only transfer->savings, while the target (residue after investments) already folded investment redemptions into surplus as income via renderBudget's totalIncome step. That asymmetry meant a redemption credited to savings inflated the target but earned zero credit on the actual side: e.g. a closed-RD redemption of 1.58L landing in savings, plus a 50,000 transfer, rendered as '50,000 of 2.12L' instead of the correct '2.08L of 2.12L'. The actual is now computed as net savings inflow = transfers into savings + investment redemptions credited to savings + income booked to savings, minus transfers out of savings, expenses paid from savings, and investment contributions funded from savings. This removes the target/actual asymmetry; in months where withdrawals from savings exceed deposits the actual can legitimately render negative. Scope is display-only: projected balances, net worth, the shared savings chain (projectSavingsChain) and all engine math are untouched. KNOWN ITEM (still deliberately deferred to its own careful build): the current-month savings forecast uses the engine's PLANNED surplus, so logging a large current-month expense does not yet re-forecast future savings (next month's plan does reflect it). That change touches projectSavingsChain and needs dedicated double-count verification before it is made. Reusable SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js remain valid for re-running all invariants from the console.
